title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. australis
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. australis, also known as the Manchurian ash, is a subspecies of the Ash tree (Fraxinus excelsior) native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior subsp. australis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and even have antimicrobial properties. Fraxinus excelsior subsp. australis can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and holistic health practices.
